experiment_type: Paper-based electrochemical biosensor for CRP detection
summary: Design, fabricate, and validate a paper-based electrochemical biosensor functionalized with anti-CRP antibodies capable of detecting C-reactive protein in whole blood at concentrations below 0.5 mg/L within 10 minutes, matching laboratory ELISA sensitivity without requiring sample preprocessing.
hypothesis: A paper-based electrochemical biosensor functionalized with anti-CRP antibodies will detect C-reactive protein in whole blood at concentrations below 0.5 mg/L within 10 minutes, matching laboratory ELISA sensitivity without requiring sample preprocessing.

references.title: Paper-based electrochemical biosensors for point-of-care diagnostics
references.relevance_note: Reviews μPAD-based electrochemical platforms for biomarker detection; does not address CRP below 0.5 mg/L in whole blood without preprocessing.

references.title: Antibody-functionalized carbon electrode for CRP quantification
references.relevance_note: Achieves 1.2 mg/L LOD on serum — threshold is 2× higher and matrix differs from whole blood.

protocol.title: Paper substrate preparation & wax patterning
protocol.description: Cut Whatman Grade 1 chromatography paper into 3 cm × 3 cm squares. Print hydrophobic wax barriers using a Xerox ColorQube wax printer at 600 dpi to define a 1 cm² working zone. Bake at 150 °C for 2 min to melt wax through paper thickness.
critical_notes: Wax must fully penetrate to prevent lateral leakage.
Allow paper to equilibrate to room temperature before functionalization.

protocol.title: Carbon working electrode deposition
protocol.description: Screen-print carbon paste (Ercon E-3449) onto the working zone using a 100-mesh stencil. Cure at 120 °C for 30 min. Rinse with DI water and dry under N₂.
critical_notes: Ensure uniform paste thickness (≈80 μm) for reproducible conductivity.
protocol.source_protocol: Ref: Nery & Kubota, Anal. Bioanal. Chem. 2016

protocol.title: Electrode activation & self-assembled monolayer (SAM) formation
protocol.description: Immerse printed electrode in 10 mM cysteamine in PBS (pH 7.4) for 2 h at RT to form an amine-terminated SAM. Rinse 3× with PBS.
critical_notes: Freshly prepare cysteamine solution — degrades within 24 h.
protocol.source_protocol: Sigma-Aldrich Technical Bulletin: SAM Formation on Carbon

protocol.title: Anti-CRP antibody conjugation via EDC/NHS coupling
protocol.description: Activate electrode surface with 0.1 M EDC + 0.05 M NHS in MES buffer (pH 6.0) for 30 min. Replace with 10 μg/mL monoclonal anti-CRP antibody (Abcam ab6) in PBS for 2 h at 4 °C. Block with 1% BSA/PBS for 1 h.
critical_notes: EDC/NHS coupling must be done in low-humidity environment.
Antibody aliquots should be single-use to avoid freeze-thaw degradation.
BSA blocking is critical to suppress non-specific binding in whole blood.

protocol.title: Electrochemical characterization (EIS & CV)
protocol.description: Characterize bare, SAM-modified, and antibody-functionalized electrodes by cyclic voltammetry (−0.2 to +0.6 V, 50 mV/s) and EIS (100 kHz–0.1 Hz) in 5 mM [Fe(CN)₆]³⁻/⁴⁻ redox probe. Confirm SAM and antibody attachment by increased Rct.
critical_notes: Each fabrication batch must be characterized before use.
Rct should increase by ≥2× after antibody conjugation.
protocol.source_protocol: Gamry Application Note: EIS for Biosensor Characterization

protocol.title: CRP detection assay in spiked whole blood
protocol.description: Spike EDTA-anticoagulated whole blood with CRP standard (Sigma C4063) at 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1, 5, 10 mg/L. Apply 20 μL sample to biosensor. After 5 min incubation, measure differential pulse voltammetry (DPV) response (−0.2 to +0.5 V, 25 mV pulse, 50 ms pulse width). Plot peak current vs. [CRP] for calibration.
critical_notes: Blood samples must be processed within 4 h of collection.
Parallel ELISA measurement required for cross-validation.

line_items.description: Antibody, EDC/NHS coupling reagents, CRP standard, BSA, redox probe
Paper substrate, carbon paste, EDTA tubes, pipette tips, microtubes
Potentiostat (Gamry Reference 600) — 2-week rental, wax printer access

timeline.name: Substrate Fabrication & Electrode Preparation
timeline.milestone: Batch of 30 electrodes ready for functionalization

timeline.name: CRP Detection & Calibration
timeline.milestone: LOD ≤0.5 mg/L confirmed in whole blood matrix

validation.primary_metric: Limit of Detection (LOD) for CRP in spiked whole blood
validation.success_threshold: LOD ≤ 0.5 mg/L; R² ≥ 0.99 on calibration curve
control_conditions: Blank whole blood (0 mg/L CRP) — must show <5% signal vs. 0.5 mg/L
Non-specific protein control (50 mg/L albumin, no CRP)
Parallel ELISA on identical blood samples
validation.statistical_method: Triplicate measurements; LOD = mean(blank) + 3×SD(blank); Pearson R² vs. ELISA
failure_modes: Non-specific binding from whole blood matrix — mitigated by BSA blocking
Electrode fouling by blood cells — monitor via CV peak shift
Antibody batch variability — characterize each lot with EIS before use
